key_points: The chapter is about a man named Kurup and his fear of snakes.
Highlights human psychology, superstitions, and fear.
The story focuses on Kurup’s experiences in a hotel and how his imagination affects his perception.
Shows how fear can distort reality and lead to unexpected consequences.
Emphasizes the theme of courage and dealing with one’s fears.
examples: Kurup imagines a snake in his hotel room even when there is none.
His fear of snakes makes him overreact to normal situations.
The story portrays how imagination and superstition amplify fear.
exam_tips: Remember the main character: Kurup and his fear of snakes.
Focus on his reactions and how imagination affects perception.
Understand the theme: fear, superstition, and courage.
Prepare to explain incidents where Kurup’s fear causes problems.
Use examples to show how human psychology is explored in the story.
